description: Canonical list of external GitHub tools. Edit this file, then run: python3 scripts/generate-external-tool-skills.py

items.summary: Python AI-assisted web scraping and structured extraction for LLM/RAG pipelines.
items.install_pointer: Follow https://github.com/ScrapeGraphAI/Scrapegraph-ai — package install, API keys, and examples in upstream README.
compliance_notes: Respect robots.txt, site terms, and applicable law; do not bypass paywalls or authentication without authorization.
Rate-limit requests; prefer official APIs when they exist.

items.summary: Claude Code plugin that captures session work, compresses it, and injects context into later sessions.
items.install_pointer: Install as a Claude Code plugin per https://github.com/thedotmack/claude-mem — upstream may ship its own skill or plugin metadata.
compliance_notes: Clarify what is stored locally vs sent to models; follow org policy for code and secrets in memory.

items.summary: Membadat is SYNTHIA's private memory repo: a branded claude-mem fork bridged into the Memory Fabric for session capture, compression, recall, and context injection.
triggers: private memory repo bridged into the brain
items.install_pointer: Install from the private repo https://github.com/marvelousempire/membadat after the fork/rebrand verification is complete; upstream attribution remains in NOTICE.md. Bridge into SYNTHIA through Memory Fabric adapters, not by vendoring source into Nephew.
compliance_notes: Preserve Apache-2.0 upstream attribution from thedotmack/claude-mem.
Clarify what is stored locally vs sent to models; follow org policy for code and secrets in memory.

items.summary: Agent orchestration platform: multi-agent swarms, workflows, RAG; Claude Code / Codex integration (per upstream).
items.install_pointer: See https://github.com/ruvnet/ruflo for deployment, MCP, and Claude/Codex hooks.
compliance_notes: Treat orchestration as production software: secrets, network exposure, and cost controls belong in operator runbooks.

items.summary: Nephew — the Orchestrator Agent by Avery Gooman. Multi-agent orchestration for Claude Code (forked from Ruflo) with an Ed25519-signed witness manifest (ADR-103), Tier B cross-repo federation, WireGuard-only mesh defaults, and per-repo `.mcp.json` set-and-forget integration.
items.install_pointer: Set-and-forget: drop the project .mcp.json at https://github.com/marvelousempire/nephew (engine currently shims to upstream ruflo until nephew@latest is published to npm). Full runbook in the consuming repo's NEPHEW.md (see family-office-platform).
compliance_notes: Private repo during bring-up — flip to public before broad adoption.
Ed25519 private key lives on the operator's machine; back up to a password manager.
Federation transport default is WireGuard; do not import tailnet patterns without explicit opt-in.

items.summary: Run Claude Code against local models on Apple Silicon using an MLX-oriented, Anthropic-API-compatible server; offline-oriented.
items.install_pointer: Follow https://github.com/nicedreamzapp/claude-code-local for hardware requirements, model weights, and server setup.
compliance_notes: Local inference still bound by model licenses and your Anthropic / third-party terms for any hybrid setup; verify NDA and export rules for your sector.

items.summary: Self-hosted generative image and video studio aggregating many third-party models (see upstream for list and terms).
items.install_pointer: Clone and configure per https://github.com/Anil-matcha/Open-Generative-AI — model keys and content policies are upstream.
compliance_notes: Generated media may be restricted by law, platform rules, and model licenses; the operator is responsible for policy.

items.summary: Official Python inference and LoRA trainer package for the LTX-2 audio-video generative model (Lightricks).
items.install_pointer: See https://github.com/Lightricks/LTX-2 and https://ltx.io/model/ltx-2 for install, weights, GPU requirements, and training docs.
compliance_notes: Upstream license is nonstandard on GitHub; read it before redistribution or commercial use.
Generated audio/video may implicate copyright, likeness, and platform policies; operator is responsible.

items.summary: Model Context Protocol bridge so agents can drive Blender scenes and operations.
items.install_pointer: Install Blender add-on / server per https://github.com/ahujasid/blender-mcp; wire MCP client to the documented endpoint.
compliance_notes: Long-running 3D operations can be destructive; confirm before batch edits or script execution.

items.summary: Open-source AI voice studio for cloning, dictation, and voice creation workflows.
items.install_pointer: See https://github.com/jamiepine/voicebox and https://voicebox.sh for builds and GPU requirements.
compliance_notes: Voice cloning has consent and fraud implications; follow jurisdiction and platform rules.

items.summary: Local voice conversation app with multiple LLM backends and pluggable TTS providers.
items.install_pointer: Follow https://github.com/bigsk1/voice-chat-ai for FastAPI/WebRTC stack and provider API keys.
compliance_notes: Microphone and transcript data are sensitive; clarify retention and cloud vs local paths.

items.summary: Docker-friendly FastAPI wrapper for Kokoro TTS with CPU ONNX and optional GPU PyTorch paths.
items.install_pointer: Use https://github.com/remsky/Kokoro-FastAPI for Docker compose, voices, and HTTP API shape.

items.summary: Pure C implementation of Voxtral TTS for lightweight embedded or server-side synthesis (per upstream).
items.install_pointer: Build from https://github.com/mudler/voxtral-tts.c — check upstream for model weights and licensing.
